skill.name: fastapi-development
skill.description: Build async APIs with FastAPI, including endpoints, dependency injection, validation, and testing. Use when creating REST APIs, web backends, or microservices.
skill.summary: Build FastAPI services with async endpoints, Pydantic validation, dependency injection, response models, and endpoint tests.
skill.author: bossjones

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code formatting, ordinary FastAPI documentation, and prior audit text in evaluation.json. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection text, or malicious intent were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the locations are Markdown code fences, inline code formatting, or quoted audit text. The only shell commands are documentation examples for uvicorn and dependency installation, not executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are documentation or strings inside JSON, not runtime code paths. No backtick shell execution construct is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert on Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms, but the referenced lines contain descriptions, FastAPI examples, previous audit reasoning, content keywords, and use case text. No hashing, cipher selection, cryptographic API use, or password storage logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows ordinary prose and FastAPI sample code. There is no cryptographic implementation to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Alert on Validation Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system reconnaissance, but the flagged lines are a simple item_id validation check and prior audit explanation. They do not inspect host data, environment variables, files, network interfaces, users, or running processes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced code checks whether a route parameter is below one. That is input validation, not host reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: FastAPI projects need clear patterns for endpoints, validation, dependency injection, and tests. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical examples for building async Python APIs.
actual_capabilities: Create FastAPI route examples with path and query parameters.
Define Pydantic request and response models for validation.
Show dependency injection patterns for shared endpoint logic.
Demonstrate database dependency cleanup with yield.
Add HTTPException based error handling for invalid requests.
Write endpoint tests using FastAPI TestClient.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not provide runnable project files.
It does not cover authentication, authorization, or token handling.
It does not include deployment, container, or production server guidance.
It uses simplified database examples without a real ORM setup.

use_cases.title: Create REST Endpoints
use_cases.description: Backend developers can scaffold FastAPI routes with typed parameters, response models, and clear validation behavior.

use_cases.title: Validate API Data
use_cases.description: Data engineers can expose data through endpoints that validate inputs and return predictable JSON responses.

use_cases.title: Test Service Behavior
use_cases.description: Full stack developers can add TestClient checks that verify status codes and response payloads during feature work.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a small service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI endpoint that accepts one path parameter and one optional query parameter.

prompt_templates.title: Add Request Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a FastAPI POST endpoint with a Pydantic request model and a separate public response model.

prompt_templates.title: Use Dependencies
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for shared endpoint logic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a FastAPI dependency that provides common query parameters and reuse it across two endpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Tested API Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task for production style review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI resource flow with validation, error handling, response models, and TestClient coverage.

output_examples.input: Create an endpoint for reading an item by id.
output: A typed route with an item identifier path parameter.
A clear response shape for the returned item.

output_examples.input: Add validation for creating a product.
output: A Pydantic model for submitted product fields.
Automatic validation for required values and numeric limits.
A response model that excludes internal fields.
Assertions for the expected status code.

best_practices: Use Pydantic models for every structured request and response.
Keep dependency functions small and focused on one shared concern.
Test both successful requests and validation failures.
anti_patterns: Do not return database objects directly from public endpoints.
Do not mix blocking I/O into async routes without a clear reason.
Do not skip response models when returning user visible data.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build FastAPI endpoints, validation models, dependencies, error handling, and endpoint tests.

faq.question: Does it create a full application scaffold?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation patterns and examples, not a complete generated project.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: No. Authentication and authorization are outside the covered examples.

faq.question: Can it help with API tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes TestClient examples for checking status codes and response bodies.

faq.question: Does it support async FastAPI code?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use async endpoint functions and explain async usage.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files contain documentation examples and no executable malicious behavior.
